query: South Korea KOSPI foreign investor net buy/sell value weekly latest June 2026, recent trend in trillion won
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the KOSPI foreign investor net buy/sell trend for June 2026:

**Headline:** Foreign investors were record-setting net sellers of KOSPI shares in H1 2026, totaling **~148–149 trillion won (~$95 billion)** — a record half-year outflow.

**Recent trend (June 2026):**
- **June:** Net sold **48.62 trillion won** (worst month; May was 44.71 trillion won)
- **June 2:** ~103 trillion won YTD, 18-session streak
- **Through June 11:** 24-session selling streak (~75.57 trillion won), 4th longest on record
- **June 12:** The lone bright spot — net **bought ~1.7 trillion won** (first buying in 25 sessions) as KOSPI jumped 8% on Middle East ceasefire optimism
- **June 19–30:** Selling resumed for 8 straight sessions
- **June 30:** **Record single-day net sell of 7.73 trillion won**

**Context:** Despite the massive outflows, KOSPI rose ~101% in H1, closing at 8,476.48 on June 30, buoyed by retail investors who net bought 99.17 trillion won. The selling was concentrated in Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix and was characterized by the KRX CEO as portfolio "rebalancing" rather than lost confidence.

**Caveat:** Sources reported monthly, streak-based, and daily figures rather than clean ISO-week weekly aggregates; the two H1 totals differ slightly (148.32 vs. 149.04 trillion won) by methodology/cutoff.

key_facts: H1 2026 foreign net selling on KOSPI totaled ~148.32 trillion won (Korea JoongAng Daily) / 149.04 trillion won (Seoul Economic Daily), ~$95 billion, a record half-year outflow.
June 2026 foreign net selling: 48.62 trillion won; May 2026: 44.71 trillion won; May–June combined 92.89 trillion won.
June 30, 2026 saw a RECORD single-day foreign net sell of 7.73 trillion won.
June 12, 2026 was the sole recent net-buying day: ~1.7 trillion won bought, first net buying in 25 sessions, on Middle East ceasefire optimism; KOSPI surged >8%.
Foreign investors net sold ~75.57 trillion won over a 24-session streak through June 11 (4th longest on record); an 8-session selling streak ran June 19–30.
January 2026 net bought ~0.15 trillion won and April net bought ~1.23 trillion won, but selling dominated the half.
KOSPI rose ~101% in H1 2026, closing at 8,476.48 on June 30; retail investors net bought 99.17 trillion won.
Samsung Electronics (~31.87 trillion won) and SK Hynix (~28.92 trillion won) were the most-sold stocks during the 24-day streak.
On June 23, KOSPI fell 9.99% (-910.71 pts to 8,203.84), its largest point drop on record, triggering a circuit breaker.
KRX CEO Jeong Eun-bo (June 11) called the selloff portfolio rebalancing after KOSPI's 76% gain in 2025, not a loss of confidence.
